errors.message:
Cloning into '/Users/ghh/Documents/Code/Code2MCP-private/workspace/dalle-mini/temp_clone'...
fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/borisdayma/dalle-mini/': LibreSSL SSL_connect: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL in connection to github.com:443

warnings:
git clone failed: Cloning into '/Users/ghh/Documents/Code/Code2MCP-private/workspace/dalle-mini/temp_clone'...
fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/borisdayma/dalle-mini/': LibreSSL SSL_connect: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL in connection to github.com:443
recommendations: Fix repository ingestion reliability first (add retry/backoff
alternate fetch methods like `git clone` fallback
and SSL/TLS diagnostics) so analysis is not blocked by zip-download EOF errors
Add a minimal Python packaging manifest (`pyproject.toml` preferred) plus pinned dependencies to improve reproducibility and tool discoverability
Create a clear dependency matrix for JAX/Flax/Transformers/Pillow (and optional Gradio/Torch/SentencePiece) with version constraints and CUDA/TPU compatibility notes
Add smoke tests for importability and MCP startup (`start_mcp.py`) to raise import feasibility above current low confidence
Implement contract tests for MCP plugin surfaces (`mcp_service.py`
`adapter.py`
`main.py`) even if no endpoints are currently exposed
Define and expose at least one health/version endpoint in the MCP layer to validate runtime wiring
Add CI (lint + type-check + unit/smoke tests) on push/PR to catch integration regressions early
Include robust error handling and structured logging in MCP startup/adapter paths for easier diagnosis in complex model stacks
Expand `README_MCP.md` with exact run instructions
environment setup
and troubleshooting for model/runtime backends
Add baseline performance checks (startup time
model load latency
inference latency
memory usage) and track them in CI artifacts
Introduce deterministic test fixtures/mocks for model calls to avoid heavy runtime dependencies in routine test runs
Add configuration management (env vars + validated settings via Pydantic) for model path/device/precision/network options
Define API/CLI entry points explicitly and document them
Improve project structure clarity by separating deployment/runtime code from generated MCP output with explicit ownership boundaries
Add security and supply-chain hygiene (dependency pinning
vulnerability scans
and trusted model artifact sourcing)
Add a fallback “degraded mode” in MCP when core model imports fail so service health checks still respond with actionable diagnostics

success_factors: Workflow orchestration remained resilient: pipeline continued after repository ingestion failure via `continue_with_empty` fallback.
Core MCP scaffold generation completed and produced expected files (`start_mcp.py`, plugin package, requirements, README).
MCP plugin runtime smoke outcome was positive (`passed: true`, service health `healthy`, stdio transport).
End-to-end node progression completed across all planned nodes (`download` through `finalize`) with overall status marked success.
failure_reasons: Primary source acquisition failed in DownloadNode due to TLS/network error (`LibreSSL SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL` to github.com:443).
Fallback repository preprocessing also failed (`zip download EOF in violation of protocol`), leaving analysis with minimal/no real source context.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing semantic understanding and confidence in generated adapter correctness.
Original project tests did not pass and had no executable test files, so functional parity with upstream project is unverified.

node_performance.download_time: Likely dominant failure point early in run; clone and zip fallback both failed due to network/TLS issues.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed quickly but with low fidelity because repository content was unavailable; outputs are metadata/fallback-driven.
node_performance.generation_time: Efficient scaffold generation; created 7 files but with low measured LOC/size metadata, suggesting shallow template output.
node_performance.test_time: MCP startup/health checks succeeded rapidly; original project tests effectively absent, limiting validation depth.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: No meaningful telemetry (reported 0 MB). Resource efficiency cannot be validated; likely low usage due to limited analysis depth.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: No meaningful telemetry (reported 0%). CPU profile unavailable; workflow likely underutilized because source processing failed.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Minimal artifact footprint (few generated files, zero reported generated size). Good for footprint, but may indicate incomplete generation metadata.
